# EnviroTime

![LPC2148](https://img.shields.io/badge/MCU-LPC2148-blue?style=flat-square)
![Build](https://img.shields.io/badge/Build-0%20Errors%2C%200%20Warnings-brightgreen?style=flat-square)
![Sim](https://img.shields.io/badge/Simulation-Proteus%208%20Pro-orange?style=flat-square)
![IDE](https://img.shields.io/badge/IDE-Keil%20%C2%B5Vision4-red?style=flat-square)
![Lang](https://img.shields.io/badge/Language-C%20%2F%20ARM%20Assembly-lightgrey?style=flat-square)

> A fully embedded real-time clock system built on the **LPC2148** microcontroller that displays live time, date, and ambient temperature on a 16×2 LCD. Supports two configurable alarms with auto-snooze, a 4-digit PIN-protected settings menu, and keypad-based navigation — no PC required during operation.

---

## Demo Screenshots

### Proteus Schematic

![Schematic](data:image/png;base64,)

> Full circuit schematic in Proteus 8 Professional — LPC2148 connected to 16x2 LCD, LM35 temperature sensor, 4x4 keypad, buzzer, and control switches.

---

### Keil µVision — Source Code

![Keil Source](data:image/png;base64,)

> Project structure in Keil µVision4 showing all source modules. Build output confirms **0 errors, 0 warnings** with code size of 13,388 bytes.

---

### Proteus Simulation Running

![Simulation](data:image/png;base64,)

> Live simulation in Proteus showing the LCD displaying real-time clock `01:49:38 26.1°C` and date `04/06/2026 THU`. CPU load at 64%, ANIMATING state confirmed.

---

## Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Real-Time Clock | Displays HH:MM:SS, DD/MM/YYYY, and day of week — live every second |
| Temperature Sensing | LM35 analog sensor read via ADC, displayed as XX.X°C |
| Dual Alarms | Two independent alarm slots (A1 and A2) configurable via keypad |
| Auto-Snooze | Unacknowledged alarm snoozes for 1 minute automatically |
| Password Protection | 4-digit PIN required before editing RTC or alarms |
| Lockout System | 3 wrong attempts locks system for 10 seconds with buzzer |
| Menu Timeout | Menu auto-exits to home screen after 10 seconds of inactivity |
| Splash Screen | "EnviroTime / Initializing..." shown on boot |

---

## Hardware Components

| Component | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| Microcontroller | LPC2148 (ARM7TDMI-S, 64-pin LQFP) |
| Display | 16×2 LCD (LM016L) — 8-bit parallel |
| Temperature Sensor | LM35 — analog, 10 mV/°C |
| Input | 4×4 Matrix Keypad |
| Audio | Piezo buzzer / sounder |
| Control | 2× push buttons (menu + alarm stop) |
| Crystal | External XTAL for accurate RTC timing |
| Power | Regulated supply with decoupling capacitors |

---

## Pin Configuration

### LCD — 16×2 (8-bit mode)

| Signal | LPC2148 Pin |
|--------|-------------|
| Data D0 | P0.8 |
| Data D1 | P0.9 |
| Data D2 | P0.10 |
| Data D3 | P0.11 |
| Data D4 | P0.12 |
| Data D5 | P0.13 |
| Data D6 | P0.14 |
| Data D7 | P0.15 |
| RS | P0.16 |
| EN | P0.17 |
| RW | GND |

### 4×4 Matrix Keypad

| Line | LPC2148 Pin |
|------|-------------|
| Row 1 | P1.16 |
| Row 2 | P1.17 |
| Row 3 | P1.18 |
| Row 4 | P1.19 |
| Col 1 | P1.20 |
| Col 2 | P1.21 |
| Col 3 | P1.22 |
| Col 4 | P1.23 |

### Sensors and Control

| Component | Pin | Note |
|-----------|-----|------|
| LM35 Vout | P0.28 | ADC channel AD0.1 |
| Buzzer | P0.25 | Active HIGH output |
| Menu Switch (SW1) | P0.7 | `#define SW1 (1<<7)` |
| Alarm Stop Switch | P0.29 | `#define SW_ALARM` |

---

## How It Works

### System Boot

On power-up, firmware initializes all peripherals in this order:

1. LCD (`InitLCD`)
2. RTC (`RTC_Init`) — skips reset if already running
3. Keypad (`InitKPM`)
4. Alarm module (`Alarm_Init`)
5. Password module (`Password_Init`)
6. ADC (`Init_ADC`)

Splash screen is shown, then control passes to the main loop.

### Main Loop Logic

The firmware runs a continuous loop with four modes:

```
MODE_RTC (0)   → Default home screen
MODE_MENU (1)  → Options menu
MODE_ALARM (2) → Alarm configuration
MODE_PWD (3)   → Password screen
```

`Alarm_Task()` is called on every iteration — alarm checking is never blocked regardless of active mode.

### Alarm Task Priority

Even while navigating menus, `Alarm_Task()` runs at the top of every loop cycle. If `g_alarm_active == 1`, the loop skips everything else via `continue` until the alarm is dismissed.

---

## LCD Screens — Every State

### Home Screen
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ 01:49:38  26.1°C │
│ 04/06/2026   THU │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Boot Splash
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ EnviroTime       │
│ Initializing...  │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Main Menu (after pressing P0.7)
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ 1:RTC  2:ALARM   │
│ 3:PWD  4:EXIT    │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Password Entry
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ ENTER PASS       │
│ ****_            │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Access Granted
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ ACCESS GRANTED   │
│                  │
└──────────────────┘
```

### RTC Edit Screen
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ 01:49:35         │
│ 04/06/2026  D:4  │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Invalid Entry
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ INVALID DATA     │
│                  │
└──────────────────┘
```

### RTC Saved
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ RTC UPDATED      │
│                  │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Alarm Menu
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ A106:30 A207:00  │
│ 1:A1 2:A2  3:BCK │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Set Alarm
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ SET ALARM1       │
│ 06:30_           │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Alarm Saved
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ ALARM SAVED      │
│                  │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Alarm Firing
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ **** ALARM ****  │
│ ALARM  06:30     │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Auto Snooze
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ AUTO SNOOZE      │
│                  │
└──────────────────┘
```

### System Locked
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ SYSTEM LOCKED    │
│ WAIT 10 SEC      │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Change Password
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ NEW PASS:        │
│ ****_            │
└──────────────────┘
```

### Password Updated
```
┌──────────────────┐
│ PASS UPDATED     │
│                  │
└──────────────────┘
```

---

## Keypad Reference

| Key | Where | Action |
|-----|-------|--------|
| `1` | Menu | RTC edit (password required) |
| `2` | Menu | Alarm config (password required) |
| `3` | Menu | Change password |
| `4` | Menu | Exit to home |
| `+` | Edit screens | Move cursor forward |
| `-` | Edit screens | Move cursor backward |
| `=` | Edit screens | Confirm / save |
| `C` | Edit screens | Clear all fields |
| `0–9` | Any input | Enter digit |

---

## Source File Structure

```
EnviroTime/
├── main.c          ← State machine, RTC display, switch detection
├── rtc.c           ← RTC init, read/write time/date/day, edit screen
├── alarm.c         ← Alarm_Menu(), Alarm_Task(), snooze logic
├── keypad.c        ← 4×4 matrix scan, GetKey(), debounce
├── password.c      ← PIN verify, change flow, lockout after 3 tries
├── input.c         ← Generic numeric digit input handler
├── adc.c           ← ADC init, Read_ADC(), voltage-to-temperature
├── lcd.c           ← Full LCD driver: StrLCD, F32LCD, CmdLCD, CharLCD
├── delay.c         ← delay_ms() utility
├── types.h         ← u8, s32, f32, u16 typedefs
├── defines.h       ← BUZZER, SW_ALARM, LCD pin macros
└── Startup.s       ← ARM7TDMI-S startup code
```

---

## Build Output

```
linking...
Program Size: Code=13388  RO-data=52  RW-data=152  ZI-data=1256
FromELF: creating hex file...
".\miniproj.axf" - 0 Error(s), 0 Warning(s).
```

---

## Tools Used

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| Keil µVision 4 | Firmware development, compilation, .hex generation |
| Proteus 8 Professional | Full schematic design and LCD simulation |
| Flash Magic | ISP flashing of .hex to LPC2148 via UART |
